Get in Touch Our Programmes

Our Mission

Serving Our Community,
Body and Soul

Christ Ambassador Ministries (CAM) is a registered South African faith-based non-profit organisation serving Port Alfred and the surrounding Eastern Cape communities through practical grassroots development and humanitarian outreach.

Founded in 1994 by Bishop Caleb Mvandaba and Apostle Hilda Mvandaba, our ministry addresses real socio-economic challenges facing our neighbours: food insecurity, unemployment, youth vulnerability, substance abuse, and limited access to skills training and mentorship.

We believe that sustainable transformation requires accountable leadership, long-term community engagement, and partnerships built on shared purpose. Our work is driven by the conviction that every person is made in God's image and deserves to be treated with dignity, hope, and compassion.

Reg. No.: 198-465 NPO  ·  Est. 1994

“
For I was hungry and you gave me food, I was thirsty and you gave me drink, I was a stranger and you welcomed me.
Matthew 25:35 (ESV)

Our Story

About Christ Ambassador Ministries

Bishop Caleb & Apostle Hilda Mvandaba

Founder & Senior Leader

Bishop Xolani Caleb Mvandaba

Bishop Mvandaba has led CAM since its founding in 1994 with a steadfast commitment to advancing the Gospel, developing principled leadership, and empowering communities through faith-based action. His leadership spans over three decades of consistent, community-rooted pastoral care in the Eastern Cape.

Co-Founder & Women's Ministry Lead

Apostle Hilda Mvandaba

Apostle Hilda co-founded CAM alongside her husband and has been the driving force behind the ministry's women's empowerment work — most notably the Sewing Project, which gives women practical skills and a foundation for economic self-sufficiency.

Christ Ambassador Ministries has been serving the Nemato township and Port Alfred area for over 31 years. What began as a tent revival in 1994 — drawing hundreds of residents and covered by the Kowie Announcer newspaper — has grown into a trusted community institution providing feeding, counselling, mentoring, and skills development to vulnerable families across the Eastern Cape.

We operate as a registered South African non-profit organisation (Reg. No. 198-465 NPO) with a physical property at 770 Runeli Drive, Nemato, and community outreach presence in Port Alfred, Bathurst, and East London.

Our ministry tagline — Ikhaya Labangcwele (The Home of the Holy) — reflects our belief that the church is not a building but a living community called to reflect Christ's love in tangible, practical ways every single day.

31+Years of Service
1994Founded
3Regions Reached
2Active Programmes

Our Work

Current Programmes

Community Feeding

Tuesday Soup Kitchen

Every Tuesday we open our doors to serve free, warm meals to youth, unemployed adults, and vulnerable families in Nemato. Demand continues to grow — hunger in our community is a real and urgent challenge, and our meals remain free to all who come.

Active Programme

Women's Skills Development

Women's Sewing Project

Led by Apostle Hilda Mvandaba, this programme trains women in professional sewing and garment production, equipping them with a sustainable livelihood skill and a pathway to economic independence. Equipment and a proper workspace are still needed to reach full capacity.

Active Programme

Daily Devotional

A Word for Today

June 2026

"The Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want. He makes me lie down in green pastures. He leads me beside still waters. He restores my soul."

Psalm 23:1–3  (ESV)

Even in seasons of scarcity — when resources are thin, when the community around us is struggling, when the road ahead seems long — God remains the shepherd who provides, restores, and leads. His faithfulness does not depend on our circumstances. Whatever challenge you are walking through today, trust that the same God who cares for the sparrow is walking beside you. You are not forgotten. You are not alone. Let Him restore your soul.

🙏 Devotionals updated monthly. Visit us Sunday mornings for weekly encouragement in person.
